summary: Phase 9.A.4 (CRT/scanline post-processing shader, toggleable) approved. Phase 9.A.4 satisfies docs/PHASES.md:580 deliverable. The shader is implemented as a CSS-overlay-only filter (no WebGL, no animation, no JS-driven render loop) so the determinism / accessibility / Phase 5 frozen contract surfaces are unaffected when off. The static scanline pattern is compatible with prefers-reduced-motion when on (no movement to throttle). Default state is 'off' — the user opts in via the toggle button. Concrete deliverables: (1) src/ui/theme/strings.ts gains 2 net-new theme registry keys: 'crtShader.toggleOn' = 'CRT scanlines: on' + 'crtShader.toggleOff' = 'CRT scanlines: off'. Total registry: 31 keys (was 29 in Phase 9.A.3). (2) src/main.ts startGame() wraps the canvas in <div id="game-canvas-wrap" class="game-canvas-wrap"> (positioning context, inline-block) + appends a sibling <div id="crt-shader-overlay" class="crt-shader-overlay"> + adds a toggle button below. Window flag __CRT_SHADER__: 'on' | 'off' | undefined declared + initialized to 'off' on startGame entry. Click handler uses canvasWrap.classList.toggle('crt-shader-on') (atomic) + sets window.__CRT_SHADER__ to the result + updates the button label via getString. New import: getString from ./ui/theme/strings (the theme-registry refactor from 9.A.3 already routed all 5 UI consumers; main.ts now joins as a 6th lightweight consumer for this single button label). (3) style.css gains the CRT shader CSS block (~50 lines): .game-canvas-wrap (positioning context, inline-block, max-width 100%); .crt-shader-overlay (position absolute, inset 0, pointer-events none, display none, repeating-linear-gradient at 1px/3px scanline spacing with rgba(0,0,0,0.28) opacity, mix-blend-mode: multiply for phosphor tint over the canvas); .game-canvas-wrap.crt-shader-on .crt-shader-overlay { display: block }; .crt-shader-toggle button styling matching the existing button family. (4) tests/ui/theme/strings.test.ts gains 1 net-new test 'CRT shader toggle keys (Phase 9.A.4 — docs/PHASES.md:580 deliverable)' pinning byte-exact the 2 new toggle button labels. (5) tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts gains 3 net-new tests: 'CRT shader toggle is visible + defaults to off' (asserts #crt-shader-toggle visible + label 'CRT scanlines: off' + window.__CRT_SHADER__ === 'off'); 'CRT shader toggle click flips the class + window flag + label' (asserts toggle on → class present + flag 'on' + label flipped; toggle again → class removed + flag 'off' + label restored); 'CRT shader overlay sits inside the canvas wrap' (asserts the DOM structure: #game-canvas-wrap contains both #game-canvas and #crt-shader-overlay). All sandbox gates green: 1194 tests passing across 83 test files (was 1193/83 in Phase 9.A.3; +1 unit test from theme registry); 0 lint errors; 0 typecheck errors; build dist/assets/index-*.js = 146.20 KB raw / 43.18 KB gzipped (was 145.56/42.97 in 9.A.3 = +0.64 KB raw / +0.21 KB gzipped from canvas wrap + toggle wiring); CSS dist/assets/index-*.css = 6.21 KB raw / 1.76 KB gzipped (was 5.61/1.60 = +0.60 KB raw / +0.16 KB gzipped from CRT shader CSS). Well under the 110 KB CI gate. Cross-runtime golden chain preserved unchanged (RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST + MAPGEN_DIGEST + SIM_DIGEST + ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed values + INVENTORY_DIGEST + WIN_DIGEST + REPLAY_DIGEST). Phase 5 frozen contract preserved: the renderer still writes to the canvas pixel buffer; the CRT overlay is a sibling DOM node that doesn't touch the canvas or sim state — toggling the shader doesn't change any state hash.
suggested_commit_message: phase-9.A.4: CRT/scanline post-processing shader (toggleable; CSS-overlay-only; default off; __CRT_SHADER__ window flag; theme-registry-routed labels)

verification_sprint.purpose: Confirm that Phase 9.A.4's CRT/scanline shader satisfies docs/PHASES.md:580 deliverable while preserving every prior-phase invariant byte-for-byte. The shader is a CSS-overlay-only filter (no WebGL, no JS render-loop, no animation); the default state is 'off' so the determinism / accessibility / Phase 5 frozen contract surfaces are unaffected unless the user explicitly opts in. The toggle is keyboard-accessible (focusable button, Enter/Space activate); the prefers-reduced-motion gate from 9.A.1 is uneffected (no animation to throttle). Cross-runtime e2e cannot run in-sandbox (Playwright browser binaries not preinstalled — established X.B sandbox limitation); the 3 new e2e tests will execute on CI's deploy.yml run.
verification_sprint.unit_tests: 1194 passed across 83 test files (was 1193/83 in Phase 9.A.3). Net change: +1 unit test from tests/ui/theme/strings.test.ts (CRT shader toggle keys byte-exact). The cross-runtime golden chain (RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST + MAPGEN_DIGEST + SIM_DIGEST + ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed expectedHash values + INVENTORY_DIGEST + WIN_DIGEST + REPLAY_DIGEST) is preserved verbatim. The 3 new e2e tests for the toggle button + overlay structure are Playwright-only (not in vitest count); they execute on CI's deploy.yml run.
verification_sprint.lint: eslint . — 0 errors. The new src/main.ts + style.css additions are within existing scopes; src/ui/theme/strings.ts conforms to the Phase 5 frozen contract (no Date/Math.random/floats — pure data + the existing single-pass formatString helper).
verification_sprint.typecheck: tsc -b --noEmit — 0 errors. The window.__CRT_SHADER__ flag is declared with a 3-state type ('on' | 'off' | undefined); the initial assignment to 'off' + click-handler assignments to 'on' or 'off' typecheck cleanly. The 2 new theme-registry keys typecheck via the StringKey union.
verification_sprint.build: vite build — green. dist/assets/index-*.js = 146.20 KB raw / 43.18 KB gzipped (was 145.56/42.97 in Phase 9.A.3 = +0.64 KB raw / +0.21 KB gzipped from the canvas wrap + toggle button wiring + 2 new theme registry entries). dist/assets/index-*.css = 6.21 KB raw / 1.76 KB gzipped (was 5.61/1.60 = +0.60 KB raw / +0.16 KB gzipped from the CRT shader CSS block). Bundle remains well under the 110 KB CI gate (~67 KB headroom).
verification_sprint.primary_workflow: User-visible addition in 9.A.4: the playable game now exposes a 'CRT scanlines: off' button below the canvas. Clicking it adds a static scanline overlay (1px dark / 2px clear, repeating vertically, with mix-blend-mode: multiply for phosphor tint) and flips the label to 'CRT scanlines: on'. Clicking again removes the overlay. Default is OFF — no user-visible change to existing flows unless the user opts in. The deterministic core / sim state / cross-runtime golden chain are unaffected (the overlay is a sibling DOM node, not a render-pipeline mutation). Cross-runtime e2e cannot run in-sandbox (established X.B limitation); the 3 new e2e tests will execute on chromium / firefox / webkit on CI.

crt_shader_overlay_implementation.evidence: src/main.ts startGame() at lines ~937-986 wraps the canvas in <div id="game-canvas-wrap" class="game-canvas-wrap"> (positioning context for absolute-positioned children) and appends a sibling <div id="crt-shader-overlay" class="crt-shader-overlay"> inside the wrap. The overlay is HIDDEN by default via CSS `display: none`; the `.game-canvas-wrap.crt-shader-on .crt-shader-overlay { display: block }` selector reveals it when the wrap has the `.crt-shader-on` class. The overlay's pattern: `repeating-linear-gradient(0deg, rgba(0,0,0,0.28) 0px, rgba(0,0,0,0.28) 1px, transparent 1px, transparent 3px)` + `mix-blend-mode: multiply` for phosphor tint. `pointer-events: none` ensures the overlay doesn't intercept canvas clicks / focus.

toggle_button_default_off.evidence: src/main.ts initializes window.__CRT_SHADER__ = 'off' on startGame entry; the button label initializes to getString('crtShader.toggleOff') = 'CRT scanlines: off'. The default-off posture preserves the determinism / accessibility / Phase 5 frozen contract surfaces unless the user explicitly opts in — vestibular-sensitivity considerations (high-contrast static patterns can be disorienting) suggest default-off is the correct posture for a v1 release. The click handler uses canvasWrap.classList.toggle('crt-shader-on') which is atomic; the boolean return value is the new state, eliminating any race / stale-state class.

window_flag_for_e2e.evidence: window.__CRT_SHADER__ declared in src/main.ts as 'on' | 'off' | undefined. Initialized to 'off' on startGame entry. Click handler sets to 'on' or 'off' based on the new class state. E2e test 'CRT shader toggle click flips the class + window flag + label' asserts the flag matches the visible state across two click cycles (off → on → off).

theme_registry_routes_button_label.evidence: src/ui/theme/strings.ts gains 2 keys at the tail of DEFAULT_THEME: 'crtShader.toggleOn' = 'CRT scanlines: on' + 'crtShader.toggleOff' = 'CRT scanlines: off'. Both labels are user-facing and would need translator/locale-overlay support; routing through the theme registry from day one (rather than hardcoding in main.ts) ensures the v1 surface is theme-mod-friendly per docs/PHASES.md:591 acceptance criterion 3. tests/ui/theme/strings.test.ts gains a byte-exact pin for both keys.

css_overlay_structure.evidence: style.css gains the CRT shader CSS block (~50 lines): .game-canvas-wrap (positioning context, inline-block, max-width 100%); .crt-shader-overlay (position absolute, inset 0, pointer-events none, display none, repeating-linear-gradient scanline pattern, mix-blend-mode: multiply); .game-canvas-wrap.crt-shader-on .crt-shader-overlay { display: block } (the toggle selector); .crt-shader-toggle button styling. The CSS is self-contained; no JS-side animation / render loop. Compatible with the prefers-reduced-motion gate from 9.A.1 (no movement to throttle).

phase_5_frozen_contract_preserved.evidence: The renderer (src/render/canvas) writes to the canvas pixel buffer; the CRT overlay is a sibling DOM node that doesn't touch the canvas or any sim state. Toggling the shader doesn't change any state hash — the cross-runtime golden chain (RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST + MAPGEN_DIGEST + SIM_DIGEST + ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed expectedHash values + INVENTORY_DIGEST + WIN_DIGEST + REPLAY_DIGEST) is exercised in Node via src/core/self-test.test.ts (4 tests pass) and tests/sim/win-replay.test.ts (7 tests pass) — preserved unchanged. tests/build/rules-files-reachability.test.ts: 7 tests pass (the 36-file reachable-set count holds; src/router/, src/save/, src/share/, src/verifier/ remain NOT reachable from src/sim/harness.ts).

tests_added.evidence: 1 net-new unit test in tests/ui/theme/strings.test.ts pins the 2 new theme registry keys byte-exact ('CRT scanlines: on' / 'CRT scanlines: off'). 3 net-new e2e tests in tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts: (a) 'Phase 9 CRT shader toggle is visible + defaults to off' asserts #crt-shader-toggle visible + label + window.__CRT_SHADER__ === 'off' on the seed-bearing URL (post-title-screen, game rendered); (b) 'Phase 9 CRT shader toggle click flips the class + window flag + label' asserts toggle on → class present + flag 'on' + label flipped; toggle again → class removed + flag 'off' + label restored; (c) 'Phase 9 CRT shader overlay sits inside the canvas wrap' asserts the DOM structure: #game-canvas-wrap contains both #game-canvas and #crt-shader-overlay. The 3 e2e tests fire on CI's deploy.yml run on chromium / firefox / webkit (9 cross-runtime assertions); the unit test runs in-sandbox.
tests_added.lines: tests/ui/theme/strings.test.ts (1 unit test), tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts (3 e2e tests)

all_sandbox_gates_green.evidence: npm run lint: 0 errors. npm run typecheck: 0 errors. npm run test: 1194 passed across 83 test files (was 1193/83 in Phase 9.A.3; +1 unit test). npm run build: 146.20 KB raw / 43.18 KB gzipped JS + 6.21 KB raw / 1.76 KB gzipped CSS (well under the 110 KB CI gate; +0.64 KB JS raw / +0.21 KB JS gzipped + +0.60 KB CSS raw / +0.16 KB CSS gzipped vs Phase 9.A.3). E2e cannot run in-sandbox (established X.B limitation); the cross-runtime golden chain (incl. REPLAY_DIGEST entry from 8.A.2a) is exercised in Node — preserved unchanged.

phase_9_a_4_acceptance_complete.evidence: Phase 9.A.4 acceptance per docs/PHASES.md:580 deliverable + the 9.A.3 approval's next_phase scope: (1) ✅ CRT/scanline post-processing shader exists (CSS overlay with repeating-linear-gradient pattern + mix-blend-mode for phosphor tint); (2) ✅ Toggleable (default 'off'; click flips on; click again flips off; idempotent); (3) ✅ Window flag __CRT_SHADER__ exposes state for cross-runtime e2e; (4) ✅ Implementation is CSS-overlay-only (no WebGL, no animation, no JS render loop) — preserves determinism + accessibility surfaces; (5) ✅ Theme registry routes the toggle button label (acceptance criterion 3 from 9.A.3 inherited); (6) ✅ Tests cover the toggle behavior (1 unit + 3 e2e, all 4 acceptance branches: visible/default-off/click-on/click-off-again, plus DOM structure).

follow_ups_carried_forward: All Phase 8 + Phase 9.A.1 + Phase 9.A.2 + Phase 9.A.3 follow-ups continue to carry forward — they're polish targets, not gating.

Code-review S1-S10 from 9.A.4 (advisory-only, all non-blocking): S5 suggests extracting the CRT toggle to a src/ui/crt-shader.ts peer module (defensible to defer until a 2nd toggle lands — the current 30-line implementation in main.ts is fine for v1); S7 suggests adding a getComputedStyle(overlay).display === 'none' / 'block' e2e pin to catch CSS drift (6-line addition; recommended for next 9.A.+ polish); S9 suggests a vestibular-tooltip on the toggle button (defer to 9.A.8 formal a11y audit); other suggestions cover doc clarification + future-proofing for WebGL post-process pipeline. None are gating.

Phase 9.A.5 (LICENSE creation) remains BLOCKED on maintainer license-choice authorization. The README.md from 9.A.1 notes 'A LICENSE file lands in Phase 9 polish; until then, all rights reserved by the maintainer.' The next iteration that reaches 9.A.5 without an explicit license preference should write artifacts/phase-blocked.json with a blocking_question requesting the choice.
Phase 9 audio + itch.io + custom-domain + atlas-recipe-tuning items remain BLOCKED on external input. None are in Phase 9 acceptance criteria; v1 ships without them.
follow_ups_observations: Phase 9.A.4 lands the CRT shader as a CSS-overlay-only filter — the simplest possible implementation that satisfies docs/PHASES.md:580 deliverable. The deliberate choice to skip WebGL post-process pipelines keeps the bundle tiny (+0.2 KB JS / +0.16 KB CSS gzipped), preserves determinism / accessibility surfaces by construction (no render loop, no animation, no JS-driven mutation), and leaves the door open for a future Phase 9 polish iteration (or v1.1 update) to swap the overlay for a richer WebGL pass via a one-element DOM swap.

The default 'off' posture is the right v1 choice — high-contrast static patterns can be disorienting for users with vestibular sensitivity, even though prefers-reduced-motion (which targets ANIMATIONS specifically) doesn't apply. A future Phase 9.A.8 formal accessibility audit may add a vestibular-warning tooltip to the toggle button.

Phase 9 progress: 9.A.1 (README + a11y CSS) → 9.A.2 (title screen) → 9.A.3 (theme registry) → 9.A.4 (CRT shader, this approval) → 9.A.5 (LICENSE — blocked) → 9.A.6 (CONTRIBUTING.md) → 9.A.7 (ARCHITECTURE.md polish + diagram) → 9.A.8 (formal a11y audit) → 9.B (live-deploy + Lighthouse > 90). v1 release ships after 9.B; project-complete.json is written at that point. Audio + itch.io + custom-domain + atlas-tuning remain deferred to v1.1 polish.

The next iteration should pick up phase-9.A.5 (LICENSE creation). Per the long-running carry-forward, this requires explicit maintainer license-choice authorization. Without that, the next iteration should write artifacts/phase-blocked.json with blocking_question = 'Which license should the project ship under (MIT / Apache-2.0 / BSD-3-Clause / GPL-3 / other)?' so the maintainer can resolve the choice externally. Alternatively, the next iteration can SKIP 9.A.5 and proceed to 9.A.6 (CONTRIBUTING.md), 9.A.7 (ARCHITECTURE.md polish), or 9.A.8 (a11y audit) — all sandbox-resolvable. The cleanest sequencing is to defer LICENSE until a v1-release-prep iteration where the maintainer explicitly sets the policy.
next_phase: Phase 9.A.5 — LICENSE creation per docs/PHASES.md:583 deliverable. **BLOCKED on maintainer license-choice authorization.** The README.md from 9.A.1 notes 'A LICENSE file lands in Phase 9 polish; until then, all rights reserved by the maintainer.' The next iteration that reaches phase-9.A.5 should: (a) check the conversation history + repo for an explicit license preference (MIT / Apache-2.0 / BSD-3-Clause / GPL-3 / other); (b) if found, create LICENSE + update package.json's license field + add a license header to a few load-bearing source files (per the chosen license's convention); (c) if NOT found, write artifacts/phase-blocked.json with `blocking_question: 'Which license should the project ship under?'` and stop. ALTERNATIVELY, the next iteration can SKIP 9.A.5 (the LICENSE choice doesn't block any subsequent sub-phase technically — it only blocks the v1 release commitment) and proceed to 9.A.6 (CONTRIBUTING.md), 9.A.7 (ARCHITECTURE.md polish + diagram), or 9.A.8 (formal accessibility audit), all of which are sandbox-resolvable without external input. Recommended: the agent loop should first try 9.A.5; if blocked, fall through to 9.A.6 and continue.
